TLDR
- Armed groups launch coordinated attacks across Mali: Multiple armed groups, including the separatist Azawad Liberation Front and jihadist JNIM, launched coordinated attacks across Mali targeting military positions in Bamako, Gao, Mopti, and Kidal. The unusual alliance between these groups has drawn close attention from analysts, with both the FLA claiming control of key cities and the Malian military reporting hundreds of militants killed. The situation remains volatile with fighting resuming in Kidal as forces attempt to drive out remaining fighters.
- Super foamy sneakers are everywhere. How do they stack up?: Chunky, cushion-laden sneakers once reserved for elite marathon runners have become mainstream street style, with celebrities like Zendaya and Harry Styles popularizing brands like Hokas, On Running, and New Balance among everyday consumers. The trend, driven by 'running club culture' and wellness lifestyle signaling, has made performance footwear a fashion statement in cities like San Francisco and Los Angeles. Exercise physiologists warn that high-stacked foam shoes may decrease lateral stability and increase ankle injury risk, though some manufacturers are addressing this with wider bases.
- India International Water Week in Sept: The 9th India International Water Week will be held September 22-26 in New Delhi, focusing on 'Climate Resilient Water Management' with participation from around 150 countries. The five-day conference organized by the Union Ministry of Jal Shakti will feature 49 sessions including ministerial plenaries, youth forums, and startup forums aimed at fostering international cooperation. Union Jal Shakti Minister C R Patil announced 11 expected partner countries for the event.
- Bus conductor's son scores perfect 625 marks, tops SSLC exam: Preetham G Poojary from Kundapur taluk in Udupi district achieved a rare distinction by scoring a perfect 625 out of 625 in the Karnataka SSLC-1 examinations. The son of a private bus conductor, Preetham credits his success to structured one-month preparation and daily revision with support from parents and teachers at Mother Theresa Memorial School. He plans to pursue Science and appear for the JEE examination, joining six other students statewide who achieved full marks this year.
- Bengaluru North commissioner flags poor upkeep of airport corridor: Bengaluru North City Commissioner Pommala Sunil Kumar conducted an inspection of the airport national highway corridor and directed civic officials to coordinate with NHAI for sustained cleanliness and intensified night patrolling. Addressing monsoon preparedness, Kumar emphasized proper drainage systems for underpasses prone to flooding, mandating all black spots and garbage vulnerable points be cleared by 8am daily with geo-tagged documentation.

Sections included:
- News regions: bangalore, karnataka, india, us, world
- Biz/Tech articles: 5
- Growth: Love, Boundaries, and Building a Life Together: A Practical Guide for the South Indian Professional About to Get Married
- Knowledge: Why Everyone Is Walking on Clouds (And Whether That's Actually Smart)
- Riddle: I have cities, but no houses. I have mountains, but no trees. I have water, but no fish. What am I?
html:
News To Me

Your Morning Paper

Edition: 2026-04-26  |  Generated: 2026-04-26T14:22:21+00:00  |  Version: 0bf32013

Read Full Edition →

TLDR

  • Armed groups launch coordinated attacks across Mali
    Multiple armed groups, including the separatist Azawad Liberation Front and jihadist JNIM, launched coordinated attacks across Mali targeting military positions in Bamako, Gao, Mopti, and Kidal. The unusual alliance between these groups has drawn close attention from analysts, with both the FLA claiming control of key cities and the Malian military reporting hundreds of militants killed. The situation remains volatile with fighting resuming in Kidal as forces attempt to drive out remaining fighters.
  • Super foamy sneakers are everywhere. How do they stack up?
    Chunky, cushion-laden sneakers once reserved for elite marathon runners have become mainstream street style, with celebrities like Zendaya and Harry Styles popularizing brands like Hokas, On Running, and New Balance among everyday consumers. The trend, driven by 'running club culture' and wellness lifestyle signaling, has made performance footwear a fashion statement in cities like San Francisco and Los Angeles. Exercise physiologists warn that high-stacked foam shoes may decrease lateral stability and increase ankle injury risk, though some manufacturers are addressing this with wider bases.
  • India International Water Week in Sept
    The 9th India International Water Week will be held September 22-26 in New Delhi, focusing on 'Climate Resilient Water Management' with participation from around 150 countries. The five-day conference organized by the Union Ministry of Jal Shakti will feature 49 sessions including ministerial plenaries, youth forums, and startup forums aimed at fostering international cooperation. Union Jal Shakti Minister C R Patil announced 11 expected partner countries for the event.
  • Bus conductor's son scores perfect 625 marks, tops SSLC exam
    Preetham G Poojary from Kundapur taluk in Udupi district achieved a rare distinction by scoring a perfect 625 out of 625 in the Karnataka SSLC-1 examinations. The son of a private bus conductor, Preetham credits his success to structured one-month preparation and daily revision with support from parents and teachers at Mother Theresa Memorial School. He plans to pursue Science and appear for the JEE examination, joining six other students statewide who achieved full marks this year.
  • Bengaluru North commissioner flags poor upkeep of airport corridor
    Bengaluru North City Commissioner Pommala Sunil Kumar conducted an inspection of the airport national highway corridor and directed civic officials to coordinate with NHAI for sustained cleanliness and intensified night patrolling. Addressing monsoon preparedness, Kumar emphasized proper drainage systems for underpasses prone to flooding, mandating all black spots and garbage vulnerable points be cleared by 8am daily with geo-tagged documentation.
